title: Massive serous cyst adenoma with ovarian abscess causing fatal septicaemia
summary:
# Summary

This case report documents a fatal complication of a massive serous cyst adenoma, a benign ovarian tumor, which became secondarily infected and progressed to overwhelming septicemia. The patient presented with a large ovarian mass that had developed an abscess, likely due to rupture or secondary bacterial infection of the cyst. The infection rapidly disseminated systemically, causing septic shock that proved fatal despite clinical intervention. This case highlights an uncommon but potentially catastrophic complication of benign ovarian neoplasms when infection occurs, demonstrating that even histologically benign tumors can cause life-threatening conditions through secondary infection.

title: Androgens (dehydroepiandrosterone or testosterone) for women undergoing assisted reproduction
journal: The Cochrane Database of Systematic Reviews
summary:
# Summary

This Cochrane systematic review evaluated the effectiveness of androgen pre-treatment (testosterone or dehydroepiandrosterone [DHEA]) in women undergoing assisted reproduction, particularly those with poor ovarian response. Testosterone demonstrated clinically significant benefits, improving live birth rates (OR 2.53) and clinical pregnancy rates (OR 2.17) compared to placebo or no treatment, with these effects proposed to occur through enhanced follicular response to gonadotrophin stimulation and increased oocyte yield. In contrast, DHEA pre-treatment showed little to no difference in live birth or clinical pregnancy rates compared to control. Neither testosterone nor DHEA substantially reduced miscarriage rates, and the evidence for effects on multiple pregnancy rates was very uncertain. These findings suggest testosterone may be a beneficial pre-treatment intervention for infertile women undergoing assisted reproduction, particularly poor responders, whereas DHEA supplementation does not appear to offer comparable reproductive advantages.
